article_meta.title: Trump strategy document revives Monroe Doctrine, slams EuropeBy Idrees Ali, Matt Spetalnick and James MackenzieDecember

reasoning: The summary stays mostly neutral, avoids the headline’s loaded “slams Europe,” and attributes charged phrasing like “civilizational erasure” to the document. Minor issues include stating that the document “has raised concern” (stronger than Reuters’ conditional) and limited inclusion of opposing perspectives.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (signals, emphasizes, reevaluation) and quotes loaded terms rather than adopting them.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: States the document ‘has raised concern’ (article framed as potential) and implies planned increases in Caribbean presence; otherwise aligned with article.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Minimal; evaluative phrases are attributed to the document.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatization beyond quoted document language.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about motives or thoughts beyond cited document aims.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasizes administration’s strategy; limited detail on critics’ views.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits noted critiques (imperialism concerns, far-right echo claims) and European officials’ skepticism about NATO timelines.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Adjectives are descriptive or quoted; ‘dominance’ mirrors article framing but is factual context.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Avoids politically charged labels; uses ‘America First’ as document framing.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balanced; does not overemphasize threats or failures.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No preferential deference; mainly summarizes official document.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than the headline’s framing and attributes charged language.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Focuses on the document; lacks coverage of analysts’ and European critics’ perspectives and lists only ‘Reuters’ as source.
